old_claim.note: Scientific Method provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim_absent: No universal scientific-method prerequisite remains because the first scoped society investigated natural secrets through mixed experimental, natural-magical, and Aristotelian practices rather than one codified method.

source_shape.source_locator: CORDIS describes the 1560 academy's experiments, natural magic, empirical Aristotelianism, and contribution to the later rise of experimentalism.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: The early society combined several investigative traditions and is itself part of the historical development of experimental practice.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Making a later generalized method its prerequisite would reverse that development and impose a uniformity absent from the institutional evidence.

ontology_before: A broad modernized scientific-method category was presumed to enable every early society devoted to nature.
ontology_after: The institutional category can begin with historically mixed investigative practices without circular method gating.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed: scientific_societies no longer directly depends on scientific_method.
would_reject_if: A primary institutional record established that membership in the 1560 Academia required the exact codified method represented by scientific_method.
short_reason: The earliest scoped society helped develop experimentalism rather than requiring one settled method.
